question: Will José Luna finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?
description:
First-round presidential elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026, with a potential second round on June 7, 2026, if no candidate receives more than 50% of the valid votes outright.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)
groupItemTitle: José Luna

question: Will the highest temperature in Shenzhen be 25°C on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shenzhen/ZGSZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a highest temperature in Shenzhen on March 22 of 27°C (31.5% implied probability) or 26°C (29.5%), reflecting the latest ensemble forecasts from models like ECMWF and GFS, which project peak highs of 26-28°C under mild southerly winds and above-normal sea surface temperatures in the South China Sea. Differentiating factors include subtle variations in afternoon cloud cover—potentially capping at 26°C if thicker stratus develops—or clearer skies boosting to 27-28°C via enhanced solar insolation and Shenzhen's urban heat island effect, which adds 1-2°C locally. March climatology averages 25°C highs, but current warm air advection from the tropics narrows uncertainty, with final odds hinging on tomorrow's high-resolution CMA updates.

question: Will Carlos Espá finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?
description:
First-round presidential elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026, with a potential second round on June 7, 2026, if no candidate receives more than 50% of the valid votes outright.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)
groupItemTitle: Carlos Espá

question: Will the highest temperature in Shenzhen be 29°C on March 22?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 22 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shenzhen Bao'an International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shenzhen/ZGSZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Chongqing's March 23 peak temperature clusters tightly around 17–21°C, driven by the latest ensemble forecasts from global models like ECMWF and GFS, which converge on a high near 19°C amid mild southerly flows. Late March climatology in the Yangtze River basin typically yields average highs of 17–18°C, but current upper-level ridging and reduced cloud cover—per China Meteorological Administration updates—favor warmer outcomes over cooler ones below 17°C. Model spread reflects uncertainty from potential afternoon convection or light showers, differentiating 18°C (top odds) as the consensus peak from edgier 20–21°C bets tied to full insolation. Historical data shows 10–15% interdaily variability, underscoring the razor-thin market edges.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tight market odds clustering 18–20°C for Beijing's March 23 high temperature stem from converging global weather model ensembles, including ECMWF and GFS, projecting mild southerly flow displacing colder Siberian air masses. Recent 00Z runs differentiate leaders: ECMWF favors 19°C with peak afternoon insolation under partly cloudy skies and low wind shear limiting mixing, while GFS nudges 20°C via urban heat island amplification in Beijing's dense core, adding 1–2°C above rural baselines. Historical late-March norms average 13–15°C, but persistent jet stream ridging drives this warmer outlook; traders monitor China Meteorological Administration hourly guidance for final convective or frontal tweaks resolving the razor-thin spread.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Wuhan's peak temperature on March 23 hinges on divergent ensemble forecasts from global models like ECMWF and GFS, which cluster implied probabilities tightly around 16–21°C amid a transitional spring front bringing variable southerly moisture and cloud cover. Official projections from China's Meteorological Administration and sites like AccuWeather peg the high near 18–19°C, up slightly from earlier cooler outlooks due to strengthening warm advection, but uncertainty persists from potential overnight cloud persistence suppressing daytime heating. Historical March maxima in Wuhan average 16–18°C with a 2–3°C standard deviation influenced by urban heat island effects, explaining the razor-thin odds separation as traders weigh model biases against real-time satellite data.

question: Bitcoin Up or Down - March 21, 7PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BTC/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Leylah Fernandez vs. Oksana Selekhmeteva: Total Sets O/U 2.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Leylah Fernandez and Oksana Selekhmeteva in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of sets completed equals or exceeds 3. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
A super tie breaker is considered as one (1) set for total set markets.
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official WTA statistics.
groupItemTitle: Miami Open: Leylah Fernandez vs Oksana Selekhmeteva Total Sets: O/U 2.5

events.title: Miami Open: Leylah Fernandez vs Oksana Selekhmeteva
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Leylah Fernandez and Oksana Selekhmeteva in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 at 11:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Leylah Fernandez' if Leylah Fernandez advances against Oksana Selekhmeteva.

This market will resolve to 'Oksana Selekhmeteva' if Oksana Selekhmeteva advances against Leylah Fernandez.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the WTA Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Leylah Fernandez's higher ranking (No. 31) and superior hard-court experience anchor her 59% implied probability against qualifier Oksana Selekhmeteva (No. 70) in this Miami Open first-round clash. Fernandez enters with steady recent form, reaching the Indian Wells third round before a tight loss to Kalinskaya, showcasing her baseline resilience and serve hold percentage above 75% on hard courts. Selekhmeteva, on a three-match qualifying win streak, lacks top-level experience against players of Fernandez's caliber, with a career 42% win rate versus top-50 foes. No reported injuries for either, but Fernandez's edge in head-to-head intangibles—none prior—and rest advantage post-bye tilt trader consensus her way amid Miami's gusty conditions favoring steady aggressors.

question: Solana Up or Down - March 21, 7PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the SOL/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: XRP Up or Down - March 21, 7PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the XRP/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance XRP/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

This market refers to the tennis match between Zizou Bergs and Tomas Etcheverry in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 24. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."

events.title: Miami Open: Zizou Bergs vs Tomas Etcheverry
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Zizou Bergs and Tomas Etcheverry in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Zizou Bergs' if Zizou Bergs advances against Tomas Etcheverry.

This market will resolve to 'Tomas Etcheverry' if Tomas Etcheverry advances against Zizou Bergs.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus has Zizou Bergs and Tomas Martin Etcheverry dead even at 50% implied probability for their Miami Open first-round clash on hard courts, balancing Etcheverry's higher ranking (No. 31) and baseline grinding style against Bergs' explosive qualifier run, where he dropped just one set to advance. No head-to-head history exists, but Etcheverry holds a slight edge in career hard-court win percentage (58% vs. Bergs' 55%), though Bergs thrives as an underdog with strong recent serve holds. Key swing factors include Miami's windy conditions favoring Bergs' big first serve or Etcheverry's superior return game if Bergs falters early; official injury reports show both fully fit, keeping odds tight absent lineup surprises.

Alcaraz enters with solid recent form, advancing to the Indian Wells quarterfinals before a tight loss to Tommy Paul, showing no injury concerns after shaking off an earlier ankle issue. While upsets occur against seeded stars on Miami's demanding courts, Alcaraz's baseline power and movement give him clear edges in rallies and return games.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alex de Minaur's surging hardcourt momentum anchors his 64% implied probability as trader consensus favorite over Stefanos Tsitsipas in the Miami Open matchup. De Minaur, fresh off a strong Acapulco final run and efficient straight-set wins over Muller and Tiafoe here, boasts superior recent form with a 12-3 record on the surface this year, leveraging his elite speed and return game. Head-to-head sits at 3-3, but de Minaur's last win in Toronto 2023 tilts dynamics on Miami's fast courts, where upsets remain possible amid fatigue factors.

This market will resolve to “Tsitsipas” if Stefanos Tsitsipas wins the first set. It will resolve to “Minaur” if Alex de Minaur wins the first set.
If the match begins but is not completed, and the first set is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on that completed set. If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50.
If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP match statistics.

events.title: Miami Open: Stefanos Tsitsipas vs Alex de Minaur
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Stefanos Tsitsipas and Alex de Minaur in the Miami Open, scheduled for March 20 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Stefanos Tsitsipas' if Stefanos Tsitsipas advances against Alex de Minaur.

This market will resolve to 'Alex de Minaur' if Alex de Minaur advances against Stefanos Tsitsipas.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alex de Minaur's surging hardcourt momentum anchors his 64% implied probability as trader consensus favorite over Stefanos Tsitsipas in the Miami Open matchup. De Minaur, fresh off a strong Acapulco final run and efficient straight-set wins over Muller and Tiafoe here, boasts superior recent form with a 12-3 record on the surface this year, leveraging his elite speed and return game. Tsitsipas advanced past gritty three-setters against Opelka and Lehecka but appears rusty post-shoulder tweak that sidelined him briefly, with no new injuries reported per official updates. Head-to-head sits at 3-3, but de Minaur's last win in Toronto 2023 tilts dynamics on Miami's fast courts, where upsets remain possible amid fatigue factors.

question: Seattle Redhawks vs. Auburn Tigers
description:
In the upcoming CBB game, scheduled for March 22 at 6:30 PM ET:

If the Seattle Redhawks win, the market will resolve to "Seattle Redhawks".

If the Auburn Tigers win, the market will resolve to "Auburn Tigers".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

events.title: Seattle Redhawks vs. Auburn Tigers
events.description:

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.

If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment has Seattle Redhawks dead even at 50% implied probability against the Auburn Tigers, largely due to Seattle University's surprising home dominance at Climate Pledge Arena—where they've won seven straight—offsetting Auburn's superior SEC pedigree and talent edge. Auburn's recent form shows road vulnerabilities, dropping two of their last three away games amid questions over starting guard Chad Baker-Mazara's ankle status from the official injury report, while Seattle rides momentum from a four-game win streak fueled by senior forward Alex Stephens' 18.5 PPG average. A confirmed Baker-Mazara return could tilt odds toward Auburn (65% historical favorite in similar cross-conference matchups), but Seattle's rest advantage after a bye week keeps it competitive; watch pre-game lineups for the tipping point.

eventMetadata.context_description: Sebastian Korda's strong hard-court pedigree and recent surge anchor his 77% implied probability against Camilo Ugo Carabelli in the Miami Open first round. The American, ranked No. 24, enters with a 10-4 record this season, including a title run in Adelaide and straight-set wins over quality foes like Zhizhen Zhang here. Carabelli, a No. 142 qualifier, has battled through three matches but owns a modest 5-7 hard-court mark lifetime, lacking firepower against top-30 power baseliners. No injuries mar either side per official reports, but Korda's 78% win rate as a heavy favorite and first-ever head-to-head tilt the matchup decisively in his favor, reflecting trader consensus on surface suitability and momentum.

question: Dogecoin Up or Down - March 21, 7PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the DOGE/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the DOGE/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/DOGE_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance DOGE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Will the highest temperature in Chengdu be 9°C or below on March 23?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/chengdu/ZUUU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Chengdu's March 23 high temperature favors 19°C or higher at 38% implied probability, closely trailed by 18°C (23.5%) and 17-16°C (22% each), reflecting convergence in global forecast models like ECMWF and GFS toward a mild spring day with peaks around 18°C. This positioning stems from Chengdu's seasonal norms—historical March maxima averaging 17-18°C in the Sichuan Basin, influenced by post-winter warming and southerly flows—bolstered by recent China Meteorological Administration data showing clearing skies after early-March rains and soil temperatures rising above 10°C. Short-term uncertainties include potential afternoon cloud cover, but ensemble models show low variance, with 95% confidence intervals spanning 16-20°C, driving bets away from extremes.

question: HYPE Up or Down - March 21, 7PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the HYPE/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the HYPE/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/futures/HYPEUSDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance HYPE/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 23 Mar '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chengdu Shuangliu International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/chengdu/ZUUU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Chengdu's March 23 high temperature favors 19°C or higher at 38% implied probability, closely trailed by 18°C (23.5%) and 17-16°C (22% each), reflecting convergence in global forecast models like ECMWF and GFS toward a mild spring day with peaks around 18°C. This positioning stems from Chengdu's seasonal norms—historical March maxima averaging 17-18°C in the Sichuan Basin, influenced by post-winter warming and southerly flows—bolstered by recent China Meteorological Administration data showing clearing skies after early-March rains and soil temperatures rising above 10°C. Short-term uncertainties include potential afternoon cloud cover, but ensemble models show low variance, with 95% confidence intervals spanning 16-20°C, driving bets away from extremes.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Shenzhen's March 23 peak temperature clusters tightly around 26–30°C, driven primarily by ensemble forecasts from ECMWF and GFS models projecting highs near 27–28°C amid persistent high-pressure ridging over southern China. Recent observational data from nearby Hong Kong Observatory shows early spring warmth with sea surface temperatures elevated 1–2°C above normal, boosting convective potential, yet incoming mid-level clouds from a weakening trough introduce downside risk toward 25–26°C. Historical March maxima average 24°C, but urban heat island effects and low wind shear favor outliers above 29°C if skies clear; key resolution hinges on 00Z model runs today, where spread in theta-e profiles differentiates the bunched 20–25% odds.
